NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED CONTINUING CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE FILED BY PLAINTIFF STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA A PUBLIC CORPORATION
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO DISMISS FILED BY DEFENDANT MILSTEIN, LEONARD R AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LEONARD MILSTEIN'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REPEATED REQUESTS FOR CONTINUANCES, ON THE GROUNDS OF PRESERVING HIS FIFTH AMENDMENT RIGHTS IN THE CRIMINAL PROCEEDINGS, EFFECTIVELY STAYED THE CIVIL PROCEEDINGS, THEREBY MAKING IT IMPRACTICAL FOR PLAINTIFF TO BRING THE ACTION TO TRIAL DURING THE TIME DEFENDANT MADE IT CLEAR THAT HE OPPOSED THIS ACTION GOING FORWARD SO AS TO PRESERVE HIS FIFTH AMENDMENT RIGHTS. BY HIS WORDS AND CONDUCT, DEFENDANT WAIVED, AND IS ESTOPPED FROM ASSERTING, HIS RIGHT TO SEEK A DISMISSAL UNDER THE FIVE YEAR STATUTE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR FEB-04-2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JUL-27-2012 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2012 Set For Trial On Feb-04-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn To Dismiss; Mtn To Dismiss; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
Off Calendar May-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert J. Perkiss, Esq., of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC, Tel. 415.989.4730, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

No other Appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LEONARD MILSTEIN's MOTION TO DISMISS is Denied. Defendant's repeated requests for continuances, on the grounds of preserving his Fifth Amendment rights in the criminal proceedings, effectively stayed the civil proceedings, thereby making it impractical for Plaintiff to bring the action to trial during the time Defendant made it clear that he opposed this action going forward so as to preserve his Fifth Amendment rights. By his words and conduct, Defendant waived, and is estopped from asserting, his right to seek a dismissal under the five year statute. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
